name: CVE Inc.
address: 1547 Merivale Road, Suite 420, Ottawa, Ontario K2G 4V3
description:
I've been a client since March and this has been the most frustrating centre to deal with. I can't get a hold of anyone using their "contact us" form, I've requested to speak to a manager regarding issues with my 'specialist'. I get told about all these programs that can help me given my limitations and then when the time comes to discuss them it crickets. Every appointment feels like the 1st of just "what are you looking for?" "What are you're barriers"... We had a specific topic to discuss at our last appointment and when I tried to bring that up was shut down, and every job interest I was told I wouldn't be chosen. Despite me talking about returning to school, interested in improving my French etc.

It sucks because I was told I can only have 3 tries with an employment agency in Ontario and the last 2 didn't help me. Prior was when I was on OW and did a weird module and had only 1 appointment, and now Cve is my 3rd... I'm screwed.

EDIT: 10-18-25

The email you gave me is the one I'm using aside from other employee emails I've been given to reach out to! You don't answer calls, and if you do I get transferred and hung up on. I also have 4 emails that haven't been responded to and that's not counting the ones to my worker. This is extremely upsetting.

I recently started my physiotherapy sessions at CVE and I’m very impressed. From the first moment, the whole team made me feel welcome and supported. The physiotherapist is extremely professional, attentive, and explains everything with great patience, which gave me a lot of confidence in the treatment. The staff is kind, organized, and always ready to help, creating a very positive and motivating atmosphere. I highly recommend CVE for anyone looking for quality care and a team that truly cares about your progress.

I want to thank Abbi at CVE Inc Employment Services for going above and beyond to help me after struggling with four Ottawa providers in 2024. She took the time to understand my needs, listened to my experiences, and truly made me feel supported. Her maturity and empathy were exactly what I needed during this process.  Abbi also transformed my resume, offering valuable expertise and dedication, despite her demanding role. She also introduced me to services I hadn’t been made aware of before. Thank you, Abbi!

I am thanking Murielo and Abbi for their amazing support and service at CVE. You guys have made helped people like myself, make dreams happen. Your company is truly incredible, that everyone no matter their background can feel supported in the workplace, like a life vest when times get tough.

I’m truly grateful!!

Krissy!

Totally recommend them. They are highly skilled at what they do. They are so professional, welcoming, understanding, patient, consistent, helpful, and simply amazing. They will help you, because they want to help. I only have positive things to say about CVE and their people. They really support you on your journey and help you reach all your career goals with respect and awareness of any personal and professional history. Thank you for making a a positive difference in my journey and our community, the world needs more people and services like you.

name: Dixie Bloor Neighbourhood Centre
address: 3650 Dixie Road, Suite 103, Mississauga, Ontario L4Y 3V9
description:
Today we had an appointment with Yuliia Holinata and she was so helpful. She did a great job assisting us with our W P application and made the process so much more smother and easier. She's a professional and really knows what she is doing. She is a really great Settlement Worker Keep up the good work. Thank you So much Respected Yuliia H 🙏

I would like to share my positive experience with  Dixie  and  Bloor  Neighbourhoid Center  where I came for help.
In particular, I want to express my sincere gratitude to Svitlana Belenko. She is truly someone who is in the right place and clearly loves what she does. She works efficiently, professionally, and with genuine care and kindness toward people.

It is always a pleasure to communicate with her and to feel her support and understanding. She creates a very comfortable and trusting atmosphere, which is extremely important in such matters.

All the reception staff are very helpful and friendly, but I would like to give special thanks to Svitlana Belenko for her dedication, compassion, and excellent work. She treats people with respect, care, and real understanding.

Highly recommended!

I highly recommend Samia Ishaq for her outstanding support with my Supervisa application.

From the very beginning, Samia demonstrated professionalism, deep knowledge of the process, and exceptional attention to detail. She guided me step by step, ensured that all documentation was complete and accurate, and patiently answered all of my questions. Her clear communication and organized approach made what could have been a stressful process feel smooth and manageable.

Thanks to her expertise and dedication, my application was handled efficiently and with great care. I truly appreciate her support and would confidently recommend her to anyone seeking immigration assistance.

I had a great experience working with Saima Ishaq at DBNC for my Super Visa invitation letter for my parents. She was patient, knowledgeable, and incredibly supportive throughout the process.

Saima made everything easy to understand and handled my concerns with real care. I’m truly grateful for her help and highly recommend her to anyone needing immigration support.

The staff at the Dixie bloor center are extremely friendly, and I had a very positive experience with them—especially Ms. Mehrnaz Emadi, who wholeheartedly tried to ease the challenges I faced as a newcomer to this country. She introduced me to the people and resources I needed in order to integrate and prepare for my professional, academic, and personal journey ahead. She helped me sincerely and with genuine dedication, and her support was truly encouraging and invaluable. I am deeply grateful to her and to the other members of the center.

name: Dixon Hall
address: 58 Sumach Street, Toronto, Ontario M5A 3J7
description:
It's a decent place to volunteer, nothing special, nothing bad either.
Help out if you can, but be prepared to be disappointed at the lack of communication and accountability the staff provides.
At least they're helping right?

Very welcoming church that is down to earth.  Started going here for Wednesday services and like how "real" it feels.  Small numbers during services but everyone is very friendly and easy to talk to.  I don't care for huge churches and this place is comforting to me.

Helped me with co-op issues.  Social worker up and dumped me in a very unprofessional manner.  I messaged her supervisor about it but she ignored me.  After receiving meals on wheels from them for a short time, I cancelled it after DAILY orders missing some of my meal.  This was a regular occurrence.  There was always something missing most days.  Food was awful.  I was treated very badly and then they just told me they couldn't/wouldn't help me anymore.  In the beginning it was great.  I'm not sure what happened.  I'm very sad and upset about it.  I'm a senior with multiple issues that I need help with.  Not sure where to turn now.  I'm very hurt.

Keep your good works going. Reaching out to  helping the vulnerable and the needy in the society. Great job.

Cannot say enough about the atmosphere and people at Dixon Hall.  If you are part of the neighborhood, stop by and see what they have to offer :)

name: Employment + Education Centre
address: 105 Strowger Boulevard, Box 191, Brockville, Ontario K6V 5V2
description:
I couldn't be happier with the team I currently are working with at the Brockville Eec location. I went through several associates at this location before connecting with Patricia and Shawn. Shawn communicates with me weekly with updates on potential positions. They both go above and beyond to assist me in my job search.

Kourtney Kelly was amazing to work with! She went above and beyond to help me with everything I needed and more! She is kind and compassionate!!! If you ever get the chance to work with her you won't be disappointed!! She is incredible!!

I’ve had a really positive experience here. At a time where I’d given up on my self the staff at the EEC have continued to help me on my path to education and employment. Thank you Kourtney for continuing to work with me and thank you for the compassion and encouragement. Thank you.

I’m really thankful for all the support I received from everyone at EEC. Each staff member helped me in different ways — from guiding me through finding a job to helping me get into college. They also taught me how to create a professional resume and gave me the confidence to use my skills.

One of the best parts was taking part in the IM&M+ (In Motion & Momentum+) workshop. It helped me discover my strengths, realize the knowledge I already had, and become a better, more confident person.

I truly appreciate everything the EEC team has done for me and would highly recommend their programs to anyone looking for help with career, education, or personal growth.

Kourtney Kelly , made my experience going through the EEC a complete breeze . She was kind and caring and overall just easy to talk to/open up to ! I would highly recommend her as a coach consular to anyone !

name: Epilepsy Toronto
address: 1425 Bayview Avenue, Suite 101, Toronto, Ontario M4G 3A9
description:
The staff are friendly, amazing and helpful.

They were funding a job coaching program for ASD through Dori Zener and this program was a big waste of time! The staff are very unprofessional

Epilepsy Toronto is a strong place and group of amazing men and women where you can get everything you need. If you don't ask you will never know. They have amazing group meetings online and and you can book individual one one one help if needed. (Assistance with : education, employment searching , government help direction, housing networking, support group support, resume updating, ect.) and I am glad that I went to Epilepsy Toronto for help.

Jesse, Carter, and Stephanie are wonderful and they are so amazing. I consider Epilepsy Toronto to be a huge part of  my life and I thank God for them. Without Epilepsy Toronto I don't know where I would be now.

The staff are very helpful and friendly.

They have helped me immensely during a hard time coming to terms with my diagnoses and sudden onset of tonic-clonic seizures on a semi regular basis. I started feeling more myself after seeing a therapist at Epilepsy Toronto, and that was a huge relief. I definitely recommend reaching out to them if you're having a hard time dealing with having epilepsy.

name: Family Services of Peel
address: 5975 Whittle Road, Suite 300, Mississauga, Ontario L4Z 3N1
description:
I had a great experience with Employment Ontario, and I truly appreciate the support I received throughout the process. With the help of Kritika Arora, everything felt more clear, organized, and encouraging. She was professional, patient, and genuinely cared about helping me move forward in my employment journey.
Kritika took the time to listen, understand my goals, and provide guidance that was both practical and motivating. Thanks to her support, I felt more confident about my next steps and better prepared for new opportunities.
I highly recommend Employment Ontario to anyone looking for career support, and I’m especially grateful for the outstanding assistance from Kritika Arora.

No ability to contact anonymously.  Calling = voicemail and personal information to be shared.

If you are grieving and need bereavement services, register with the Centre for Grief and Healing with the Bereaved Families of Ontario Halton/Peel instead. Always ask for full names and research "therapists" before you agree to meet with them. Agencies should be protecting the public by not continuing to let staff provide services that they are clearly not fit for.

My husband and I attended for a marriage counseling session.  After a 30 min wait we were told his was " unable to attend"    We were called by same individual and AGAIN - HE was a no-show...total waste of time..However,  my Toronto Doctor referred us to a top notch,extremely well educated individual.   She took us as she was so distressed by Pell Family Services behaviour.   Catholic Family Services offer courses through CMHA (non catholic).  Maybe for Employment they can handle it but I don't hear anything positive about there " walk in services".  I'd rather try any place but them.

Am really happy to be part of the program with the Employment Ontario and look forward in telling my friends about them so they to can come and learn. Today I worked with Kritika Arora and she really gave amazing strategies and drafted my resume as well.
Highly recommended

name: Job Skills
address: 1325 Eglinton Avenue East, Unit 213, Mississauga, Ontario, L4W 4L9
description:
I had an amazing experience at job skills agency. TJ, Julia and Vishwa helped me with my résumé and were honestly incredible — patient, supportive, and really understood what I was trying to achieve. They helped me bring out my strengths and boosted my confidence. I’m truly grateful for their help and would recommend them to anyone.

I had a great experience working with Carol and the team in the Mississauga office. Carol was extremely helpful, patient, and professional throughout the whole process. She helped me create multiple versions of my resume and even found job leads that matched my experience perfectly. I highly recommend their services to anyone looking for guidance or support with job searching.

I had a fantastic experience working with Carol. She went above and beyond to help me find a job that aligned with my skills, capabilities, and business knowledge. Her insights and advice on broadening my search area were incredibly valuable and made a real difference in my job hunt

Today they had an event to help people find jobs with growing companies who are in the need of people who are and share experiences they are looking for! Pretty cool experience!! And just want to say thanks to the employees of job skill and the REST Centre for this opportunity:)!

I attended the RFY program for 12 weeks in Mississauga, hosted by Tetiana Sokurenko. I have nothing but good things to say and am very grateful for this opportunity. It’s also a very welcoming office environment. VERY MUCH NOT SCAM!

name: Job Skills
address: 4961 Highway 7, Unit 100/101, Markham, Ontario L3R 1N1
description:
I cannot recommend Miss Oksana Chjen at Job Skills enough! She is truly an exceptional and reliable professional who genuinely cares about the people she helps. From the very beginning, she has been incredibly supportive, kind, and dedicated to making a difference.

What makes her stand out is how much she values the people she works with. Even after a couple of years since our first meeting, she still remembers me and continues to reach out ,check in, and provides updates about opportunities and services that may help. That level of commitment and follow-ups is rare and truly meaningful.

Miss Oksana Chjen always goes above and beyond to assist in any way she can. Her kindness, patience, and willingness to help make her one of the most genuine and compassionate people I have ever met. You can truly feel that she cares about your success and well-being.

I am very grateful for all her support and guidance. If you are working with Job Skills, you are very lucky to have someone like Miss Oksana Chjen on your side.

I’m sharing my story because I truly hope it encourages others who are on their
job search journey. I want to give a massive thank you to Job Skills and my
specialists, Oksana Bachynska and Daniel Dong, who helped me land my new job
and start a new life.
From our very first meeting, they were so patient. They didn't just look at my
experience; they took the time to understand my current situation, my strengths,
and where I wanted to go next. They coached me on how to build a resume to
show my unique value.
Oksana and Daniel worked hard to find roles that were a real fit for me, constantly
recommending my profile and securing interviews. But they didn't stop there—
they coached me on the nuances of Canadian workplace culture and how to
present my full potential to employers during those short interview windows. Even
after I accepted the offer and started working, they kept checking in to see how I
was settling in.
The entire journey—from the day I registered to the day I got the offer—took only
80 days. Their patience, efficiency, and professional wisdom are honestly beyond
words. They have an incredible intuition for what both candidates and employers
need, and they always gave me the right advice at exactly the right time.
If you’re looking for help from Job Skills, my best advice is this: trust your
specialist. Be honest with them, follow their professional guidance, and put in the
work. The surprise will come to you soon.

I recently joined the Simpact Practice Firm Program, and it’s been such a great experience! The program has really helped me improve my job search, resume writing, and interview skills.

Big thanks to Nikki and Elizabeth for all their support. They run different workshops every day, and each one teaches me something new and practical. I’ve learned so much from them, and their encouragement makes a huge difference.

I’m really glad to be part of this program — it’s been super helpful and motivating!

Lawrence Gomes is my employment consultant. I'm deeply thankful for his guidance and support for my job search in this very tough job market. He has helped me refine my resume, recommended me to register for online courses and programs, such as the JITA program at NPower Canada, to refine my skills and education, and encouraged me to apply for job posts, workshops, and job fairs that align with my current set of skills. His guidance has really helped me stay productive and more motivated in the job search. He has also offered a mock interview with me to ensure my success, and his advice and feedback have been very helpful.

Job Skills offers the best support to those who are struggling to look for a job. I am also grateful to Carol and Kevin for making a positive impact on my journey.

The practice firm offers a warm and supportive environment where jobseekers like us coming from different backgrounds can build real skills for meaningful employment. Through the 8-week program, we gained hands-on experience in administration, accounting, marketing, and Canadian workplace culture, while also learning how to work with people and adapt to different workplace environments, and forming valuable professional connections.

I am truly grateful to my employment counsellor, Ms. Hazelann, for her continuous encouragement and prompt support. As soon as she learned that a position in the ECA course became available, she reached out to me right away to make sure I didn’t miss the opportunity to apply. Her thoughtfulness and dedication mean a lot to me.

Thank you to both my facilitators： Nikki Rai and Elizabeth Javier, for ensuring that our weekly sessions were always productive and insightful. Through exchanging ideas and learning together in the classroom, we became a group of individuals with the same goal—gaining meaningful experience and successfully finding employment. Your guidance helped us stay motivated, connected, and confident in our journey.

Finally, I am truly grateful to Elizabeth Javier. Through her support, I was connected to the job developer, Kevin Lam, who then referred me to a job opportunity in a restaurant—something I had never experienced before. This opened a new door for me, and I appreciate their trust and encouragement.

Swee Peng Joanne Chan

name: Job Skills
address: 155 Riverglen Drive, Unit 7, Keswick, Ontario L4P 3M3
description:
I've had a really great experience with Job Skills Keswick! The staff are super friendly, welcoming, and genuinely want to help you succeed. They helped me update my resume, prepare for interviews, and gave me a lot of useful advice about the job market, as well as set realistic expectations. I felt supported every step of the way. You can tell they really care about people who want help. If you're looking for work or even just some guidance on your career path, I definitely recommend checking them out!

I was very disappointed with my experience at Job Skills, especially at the Keswick location. Unfortunately, the program turned out to be a complete waste of my time and energy. There was no meaningful support, follow-up, or real understanding of client's situation.
The consultant I worked with seemed inexperienced and uninterested.
I joined the program with hope, but left completely discouraged and decided to close my file. I truly hope Job Skills, especially the Keswick branch, improves its level of professionalism and care toward clients in the future.

When I suddenly found myself unemployed after years of working in the same industry, Job Skills made navigating this terrain almost enjoyable.  Services offered, included Resumes for 2025, Interview Techniques, and even 1 on 1 coaching sessions.  Special thank you to Charlene - for going above and beyond.

I want to express my gratitude to Michal, the resource advisor and receptionist at Job Skills. Her kindness, professionalism, and willingness to go above and beyond—such as reviewing resumes on short notice—have made a significant difference in the job search process. Michal's warmth and genuine support create a welcoming environment for job seekers, making what can be a challenging process much easier.

I would also like to acknowledge Alice, who has been a great listener and an incredible support. Her people skills and genuine care make a lasting impact, further enhancing the positive experience at Job Skills.

Their dedication and care reflect positively on the entire Job Skills team, who have consistently provided great support. Thank you for all the assistance. Your efforts are truly appreciated. I highly recommend Job Skills to anyone seeking career guidance and resources.

Julianna, thank you so much! I'm really impressed with how fast you completed my resume and how good it looks. I truly appreciate your help!

name: Job Skills
address: 70 Innovator Avenue, Unit 5, Stouffville, Ontario L4A 0Y2
description:
This job skills website is very well designed and easy to navigate. The layout is clean and professional, making the content clear and accessible. Everything works smoothly, and the overall presentation reflects a high level of quality and attention to detail. It provides a great user experience and leaves a strong, positive impression.

I have been working with job skills Stouffville office for a while. Always feel welcome here and get tons of help from my consultant Fatima, and my job developer Sandra, Oksana through different stage of job search . They helped me land a job offer recently, which is a perfect match for my skills and goals. Big thanks to the JS Stouffville team! Will highly recommend for anyone who lives in the area and wants a job!

Not useful. Don't waste your time. Job skills can't help you find a job and the training is very poor. They don't have any contacts as they try to tell you. I think they just want to put you on their books so they get money from the government. Wasn't good at all.

Definitely recommend every job seeker to check out their services. Sandra Fagundes and MacKenzie Bellows really helped me out update my CV and prep me for Interviews. I landed a full-time job because of their help!

Their customers service is beyond excellent and they are all so pleasant and nice. They go above and beyond to make you feel comfortable and succeed.

name: Job Zone d’emploi – Stormont
address: 144 Pitt Street, Cornwall, Ontario K6J 3P4
description:
Good morning do not use this service if you are a single dad. They are there to fill there pockets it’s a government funded program lol. I sit down with a worker laughs at me for not having a job I said that’s not very professional she says sorry after she realized what she did wow. Then she says here are some job sites, she also said I will not find you a job, I said explain please well here are some job sites good luck. Then I send a email the next day it says out of office for 2 weeks okay I get you need a break but just tell me. I contact the supervisor and she was even worse just protecting the employee wow again. If you need to find work you don’t get help as a Single dad I will keep on doing my best but just rember there is like 20 offices and here is what they say keep applying. The question have is a tax payer how come there is no ramifications for people on the government to help Canadians just cancel the program we would save millions O did call the management and all she did was take the side of the employee,what a terrible terrible organization all smoke and mirrors. I spoke with the Director what a joke I am sorry she says that’s all you will get. The program is like any program here to help everyone but White Single Dads born in Canada. If you are a single dad you have to hustle on your own theses female bosses laugh at us and that’s okay. It’s not okay we need to stop using these programs and let them have yo hustle. They are not there to help they are there to make sure the refugees and Immigrants find work not me or you. She starts integrating me I am the one making the complaint.She doesn't care about us only her laughing staff that laughed at me for not having a job. I will continue to comment on this place through social media, there is to many bad bad people working in our community. Please Do Not use Job Zone Cornwall they are not good or professional.

Great service and understands the situation of the Candidate with directions to suit in good job placement , based on  employment Industry standards according to quality service . Kristy did a wonderful service to me with directions to tackle the situations .

Great service
Receptionist very nice and cooperative.
Special thanks for Mr Arthur for his guidance

I want to take a moment to thank Arthur de Oliveira Corrêa and Linda, the woman at the front desk, for their exceptional support at Jobzone. From the start, both went above and beyond to assist me, and they've been consistently helpful every single time I've had an appointment. Each visit was productive and brought me one step closer to my goal of finding employment.

Arthur in particular was a huge help. He always made sure I was informed about upcoming job fairs, guiding me on how to attend and even offering advice on how to dress for success. Their assistance with funds was incredibly helpful as well, and I truly appreciated the financial support they provided.

Both Arthur and Linda were friendly and made me feel welcome at every appointment. They were quick to respond to any questions I had and made the whole process feel personal and supportive. I couldn’t be more grateful for their dedication and kindness.

Thank you, Arthur and Linda, for everything! You've made this experience a lot smoother and more encouraging. I appreciate the support more than I could ever explain and look forward to starting my new job on Monday!

I just went to JobZone this morning to write out a resume and cover letter. The help from their experienced staff exceeded my expectations. I was dreading having to start this process over from scratch, but I now have exactly what I needed. I wish I had gotten the man’s name who helped me but huge thank you to the staff at JobZone!

name: JVS
address: 74 Tycos Drive, Toronto, Ontario M6B 1V9
description:
I was really excited to receive a call for this role, especially so soon after applying. Unfortunately, I later learned that the position is government-funded and requires candidates to be Canadian citizens or permanent residents. This eligibility requirement wasn’t mentioned in the job posting, which was disappointing, as it raised expectations and took time on both sides. I hope future postings can include this information upfront to help candidates better assess their fit.

Terrible I went to a job fair and even signed up for it online. I wasted almost an hour of trying to get there and bus fare just to be told that I can not even go to the job fair cause I was a client for YMCA. What was the whole point of signing up for it just to get denied for it! waste of time

I went to JVS Al Green Resource since 7 year ago, I showed inappropriate video to Participate for Second and Third They kick me out Al Green Resource. They don't know my Behavior towards people. I wait to force got to counseling for no reason for purpose of the video to Participate. I'm not a rude person to people.

IF YOU HAVE A DISABILITY DO NOT GET HELP FROM THESE PEOPLE. They do not have the resources to help people with disabilities and will not get you a job, let alone an accesible one. Theyre extremely unprofessional, disorganized and abelist & I also dealt with transphobia while attending the workshops. Save yourself the time and stress and find any other employment agency.

They present well but won’t help you, I went there 2 years ago and they did nada! After when I sent them a reminder/follow up email they replied asking me to go to LinkedIn and find a job. My email was polite and just a follow up after months and their reply was very arrogant,

name: JVS
address: 1280 Finch Avenue West, Suite 607, Toronto, Ontario M3J 3K6
description:
I had a very positive experience with JVS, and I’m truly grateful for the support I received during an important transition in my career in Canada.

I would especially like to thank Mohsen Jafari Gharaghani for his outstanding support and professionalism. Mohsen was extremely knowledgeable, patient, and genuinely invested in my success. His guidance and encouragement made a real difference and helped me navigate this process with much more confidence and clarity.

JVS offers practical, high-quality support for newcomers and professionals building their careers in Canada, and I would highly recommend their services to anyone looking for trusted guidance and real results.

Thank you again to the JVS team, and especially to Mohsen Jafari Gharaghani, for all your help and support.

My experience with the ISAGE program at JVS Toronto was truly life-changing. The program provided me, as a new immigrant, with the foundational support and strategic guidance needed to successfully establish my career in Canada.

I am deeply grateful to Orcun Unsal, Mohsen Jafari Gharaghani, and Hamideh Damghani for their exceptional mentorship. Their insights gave me the confidence and skills to achieve a key professional goal: securing a dream job that I had previously thought unattainable.

Furthermore, the program's greatest gift was the sense of community it fostered. I am thankful to have gained not just career success, but also lifelong friendships. For any new immigrant looking for a program that invests in your whole success, ISAGE is the definitive choice.

My sincere thanks to the entire JVS Toronto team.

I highly recommend JVS Toronto. The Workforce Specialists, Employment Counsellors and Mentoring Coaches provided personalized coaching to help me present my international experience for the Canadian job market, offered practical supports, and connected me with employers. I found work by the program’s end. Their services are practical, respectful, and effective for newcomers.

The JVS Toronto Organisation is very exceptional. I contacted them just 1 month after landing into Canada as a newcomer. I got an apppointment and was assigned an employment counsellor which guided me through settlement for internationally trained professionals. Through one of their programs I enrolled called ISAGE, this provided me with more in-dept knowledge in the field of Social Work. The program gave me extensive training and skillset to integrate into Social Work Practice in the Canadian context . I was able to get a full time job in  2 months while in the program, and it was really worth the time and brought me great success. I strongly recommend JVS Toronto.

I had an excellent experience with JVS Toronto North. Mohsen was exceptional, supportive, knowledgeable, and always willing to go the extra mile to help me succeed in my job search. His guidance played a big role in helping me secure a job.
And a special thanks to Sahar as well. Sahar, was always kind, encouraging, and provided helpful guidance throughout the process.
Thank you to the team for all your support!

name: JVS
address: 2868 Ellesmere Road, Scarborough, Ontario M1E 4B8
description:
I had a fantastic experience with JVS, thanks in large part to my counsellor, Abinaya. She provided guidance, resources, and encouragement throughout my job search, making the process much smoother. I’m very grateful for her support and highly recommend JVS to anyone seeking career assistance.

Unfortunately a lot of agencies like these ones get subsidised by the government to "find you a job" but the problem is the all pocket the money and pay their employees to keep you on the fish hook enough to collect.

I had a chance to get the employment councillor services from JVS Scarborough location in March just before pandemic shut down everything for which i had asked for in February though. The lady I spoke to as my EC was phenomenal, kind and supportive back then when we firsttalked.

let's see what she finds for me, so this is my experience of almost 8 months. In the very first meeting I came to know around 80% of jobs are come through these employment agencies. However, its been 8 months now i haven't heard any job information from my EC which is quite inefficieny from JVS. The job I got was from indeed to behonest. So I don't know how they work, they don't reply to emails its like I had very bad experience with them.

Exceptional service; I've been with many other employment aid services such as YES and Springboard, and while helpful, I've found that the results gained haven't been as significant as with JVS; I've been offered and acquired opportunities far greater than I've ever had before.

I would like to give a special shoutout to Judah for his amazing guidance in aiding me with perfecting my resume and cover letter.

They don’t respond to emails on time or at all. There will be days that I don’t hear back from them, they don’t update or set up appointments to meet you, you just have to be the one to remind them that you still exist. Would not recommend.

name: JVS
address: 1911 Finch Avenue West, Unit 3, North York, Ontario M3N 2V2
description:
Today I would like to leave a very sincere review for JVS Jane Finch Mall location and especially for my employment counsellor Sonia Marroquin. For JVS: you have my name in your database, so to make sure that my review is not fake, and nobody asked me to post it, you can always reach out to me by calling my number.

Before coming to JVS, I was a client at three other employment agencies sponsored by Employment Ontario. Unfortunately, I did not receive the service I was looking for. As an example, in one agency, my employment advisor was changed three times within about one to one and a half weeks, without explanation. Each time, I had to fill out my application again and explain my situation from the beginning. It was stressful and not a good experience.

At JVS, I had to change my employment advisor once as well, but the difference was how professionally and respectfully this was handled. With the help of Sumika Takahashi and Michelle Frazer, they carefully matched me with a counsellor who truly fits my needs. That is how I started working with Sonia, and it made a big difference.

Sonia always responds to my emails, gives clear and professional advice, and helps me understand what support I am eligible for before I start working. She explains things patiently and makes sure I understand my options. She never rushes conversations and never makes me feel like just another client or number.

Working with an employment counsellor does not mean they find a job for you. It means they support you when you are putting in effort, guide you to the right resources, and help you ask the right questions. Sonia does exactly that. I came to JVS with a very specific request, and she understood it from the beginning.

I have lived in Toronto for twelve years, and English is … still my second language. 🥲 Anyone who has this experience knows how difficult it can sometimes be when people hear your accent or realize English is not your first language. With Sonia, I have never felt misunderstood, gaslighted or mistreated. She treats me with respect, kindness, and professionalism.

Before the Christmas holidays, I wanted to leave this review as a thank you. I have been responsible for everything in my life here, from learning English to paying bills and building my future. Sonia is one of those professionals who makes you feel supported instead of lost.

She listens, she answers questions, and she provides realistic and personalized help instead of just telling you to apply for more jobs. She is still my employment counsellor at JVS, and I truly appreciate her work.

Thank you, JVS Toronto, for having professionals like Sonia who genuinely care about their clients. Happy Holidays! 🙏

Went here, but got a job on my own; they helped with transit, for interviews. Moreover, during my intake, they mislead about giving me financial support for training costs(Uniform, etc). I wasted bus fare two days in a row to go there: trying to convincing them I got the job and I need support, to get on my feet(Uniform cost), from here-on-in. They didn’t believe I got a job: I showed them paystubs and date of hire nothing was compelling.

This place doesn’t add up, they receive funding from the government to help locals and newcomers, but lack incentive to actually help with the tangibles. They may appear nice, but doing the grit and spending the money government gives to help locals doesn’t interest them. This review is my first stop, next up personnel in Doug Ford cabinet. To cut funding here, if there no going to provide any results; the mall(Jane & Finch) is ridden with unemployed people, in state of malaise. Help people!

Chanice is really good at JVS as well! Her and David deserve promotions in my opinion! Oh yeah; lets not forget Sumika as well! She helped me retain progress on an application that I am working on with a few others! Yes "head office"... I am talking to you! These folks deserve a RAISE!

I saw JVS Toronto on CP24 and reached out to them, they are very professional and know what they are doing. I would recommend them to anyone looking for help when it comes to building your resume and career into the right path. They have the most amazing staff, very friendly, knowledgeable and professional. Amazing!!! Amazing!!! Amazing!! Amazing!!!

Youth worker doesn't respond to calls. My son needed support for his first resume and. Job. The youth worker totally ignored our calls and messages.
When people wanna do good it seems they nerd to help with quotas to keep crime rates up.  But they help others they have preference for. We got help from another organization instead. WICKED

name: JVS
address: 184 Front Street East, Suite 203, Toronto, Ontario M5A 4N3
description:
Mina is an exceptional Employment Counsellor and a tremendous asset to JVS Toronto.  She immediately put me at ease, quickly understood my employment history, and empowered me to take decisive next steps with clear direction.  I was particularly impressed by her creative problem-solving skills which helped me navigate a hurdle, that previously seemed impossible to overcome.  I also appreciate the gentle way in which Mina helps her clients see the bigger picture and make informed decisions, through sharing personal examples from her experience.

Mina’s expert guidance on résumé optimization, interview preparation, networking, and the effective use of online tools strengthened my confidence and positioned me for success.  She consistently provided excellent ideas and sound advice that kept me moving forward in the best direction.  Mina is a highly intelligent, strategic and deeply dedicated Employment Counsellor who truly goes above and beyond for her clients.  I am sincerely grateful to Mina, Marisa, Dhruv and the JVS team for their support.

Leyla Ahmadova is an outstanding Employment Counsellor who genuinely cares about her clients’ success. She is knowledgeable, patient, and highly supportive throughout the job-search process. Leyla provided clear guidance on résumé improvement, interview preparation, and career planning, which helped build my confidence and direction. Her professionalism and encouragement made a real difference. I highly recommend her to anyone seeking employment support.

From start to finish, my counsellor Saif was exceptional. The experience went above and beyond my expectations. Saif was extremely knowledgeable, empathetic, professional and insightful. After just one session I feel more confident and supported.  A special mention to Marisa who was also very helpful in getting my appointment set up.

I had an absolutely incredible experience here with Leyla! Not only did I feel equipped for my job search but also secure with moral support! The job hunt is a brutal one and to not only have someone get you set up and land interviews but to be almost a therapist?! Incredible. Highly recommend to everyone looking for a push and to find tune their job hunt strategy!

I`ve been trying to get into the Better Jobs Ontario program with this location since October 2025. When I have an appointment with the guy named 'Saif' he cancelled at the last minute because he was 'sick'. For some reason, they can`t pass my file onto another rep in the office. That`s very lazy. I had 4 appointments cancelled with this office in almost 3 months because of the lack of over all communications. Rosalia their 'Manager' (which is a stretch), didn`t help matters at all. Only things she can really do is confirm that there are cancelled appointments or no appointments

Very lazy people at this office. Steer clear of this place. They`re (odds are) like all other job placement centers, they suck up a pay cheque while playing solitaire or check their social medias for 8 hours.

name: JVS
address: 30 Karachi Drive, Unit 66, Markham, Ontario L3S 0B6
description:
Truly Supportive and Professional Team!

I had an excellent experience with JVS. The whole team is caring, professional, and dedicated to helping clients achieve success. My counsellor Laura Sun and job developer Lakshimi Naharajan have been absolutely amazing — they supported me every step of the way, from preparing for job applications to adjusting to my new role.

Laura is patient, understanding, and always provides thoughtful guidance. Lakshimi is proactive and genuinely committed to ensuring her clients succeed — she checks in regularly and offers helpful advice, resources, and encouragement.

JVS’s employment program is well-structured and full of useful support, including resume and interview preparation, continuous follow-ups, and even practical assistance like transportation and work-related supports.

I’m truly grateful for their help and highly recommend JVS, especially Laura and Lakshimi, to anyone looking for professional, compassionate, and effective employment support.

I was only with JVS for a few weeks but I got all the support I needed and managed to get a job thanks to my employment counsellor Aatika Bhatti and Work Force Specialist, Team Lead: Lakshimi Naharajan. With their support and help with feedback on my resume and helping me with mock interviews. They also encouraged me to join the JVS workshops and Job Fairs so I could gain more confidence. I managed to get a job offer in less than 3 weeks.

I needed help with my application with jobs Ontario so I came to JVS.
Staff was not helpful. She said you must attend our Information session 1st which happens once a month. So you can attend it next month when it happens. Then she asked me to go to Job Skills which would offer the same services and they might be able to assist you with your application for Better Jobs Ontario.
It is very frustrating when you are looking for help and centres which are meant to help you also divert you to go somewhere else.
I am very disappointed with JVS Toronto Employment Source Markham

I recently had the pleasure of working with JVS Toronto Employment Source Markham, and I can’t say enough good things about my experience. A special shout-out goes to Lakshmi, who went above and beyond to provide outstanding support and guidance throughout my employment journey. Her dedication and professionalism truly made a difference.I got a job within acouple of weeks from my registration!
I also want to extend my appreciation to  Sacha who refined my resume and the administration team for their wonderful service and attention to detail. The entire team at JVS Toronto Employment Source Markham is genuinely committed to helping clients succeed, and it really shows in their work.
The following up even after I got the job, was amazing and  reflected how much they care.
Thank you for the excellent experience!
I highly recommend JVS!

Sacha Abrahamse is an excellent Employment Counsellor - very supportive, professional, and helpful throughout my job search. Thanks to her guidance, I found the right opportunity. Highly recommended!

name: KEYS Employment and Newcomer Services – Gananoque
address: 375 William Street S, Gananoque, Ontario K7G 1T2
description:
I am a business owner and was a little stuck with no printer on site and nothing close by.
I came in and asked for assistance and the people at the front desk were amazing
Keys job center is very helpful.

I want to thank Keys and Emily for such a warm welcoming. I am excited to work with Emily and learn more about the job seeking process. Thank you for being a breath of fresh air and also for your encouragement.

I called asking for help with my resume and job searching and nobody answered the phone. I left a message asking them to call me back and they never did. What's the point of this company if they don't do what they are supposed to help us with?

Received zero help

Highly unimpressed.

name: LDATD
address: 2 Lansing Square, Suite 506, Toronto, Ontario M2J 4P8
description:
I had a great experience with the Working at Heights training at the Worksite Safety North York Training Centre. The class went very well, and our trainer, Akil, was excellent. He was very friendly and approachable, which made the training engaging and enjoyable. He explained everything clearly and thoroughly, making the material easy to understand for everyone. He also encouraged participation and made sure all questions were answered. Overall, it was an informative, well-organized, and enjoyable training session. I would highly recommend this course and instructor..

I had a great experience doing my Working at Heights training with Worksite Safety. The course ran from 8:30 am to 4:00 pm, and the time was managed perfectly.
The training was a solid mix of written material and hands-on experience, which really helped solidify what we were learning. A special thanks to our instructor, Akil. He was knowledgeable, kept the class engaged all day, and made sure everyone was comfortable with the practical gear. Highly recommend!

I really enjoyed this training. The instructor Ryan delivered the material with great enthusiasm and made the session very engaging. The information was very useful and well explained. I especially liked how he included jokes and short videos during the training — it made everything more interesting and easier to understand. Overall, it was both informative and enjoyable. Thank you very much for the great training!

I had the pleasure of attending the Working at Heights training conducted by Mr. Akil Jiwani. His session was engaging, practical, and very informative. He explained every concept clearly and made sure we understood the safety procedures through hands-on activities. Mr. Akil’s teaching style made the learning experience both professional and enjoyable. I truly appreciate his guidance and highly recommend his training sessions.

Akil is an excellent Working at Heights trainer. He explains safety requirements clearly, uses real worksite examples, and makes sure everyone fully understands proper procedures. The training was well-organized, practical, and focused on real-world safety. I feel more confident and prepared to work safely at heights after this course. Highly recommended.

Such a wonderful and inclusive community to be a part of! The employment and community support team staff are incredible, and the space they have created together to support the individuals who access their program
are truly one of a kind!.

An amazing organization! The dedication to their mission is total and the commitment they show on a daily basis is inspiring.

A fantastic organisation that's devoted to meeting people where they are and helping them achieve their goals.

Counsellor is very patient and personable

name: Newcomer Women’s Services Toronto
address: 355 Church Street, Toronto, Ontario M5P 2G7
description:
Joining the Newcomer Women’s Toronto ALP program has truly been one of the best decisions I’ve made.  it’s been a lifesaver!

You’ll never feel lost with this amazing community of cheerleading sisters by your side. Each woman brings her own strength — from Amrita (your go-to for resumes, interviews, and cover letters) to Nastaran and Natalia (who lead inspiring panel sessions), and Saima, Sehr, Banoo, and Misbah, all helping you find the right opportunity for you and  offering support in every possible way.

The study courses were phenomenal, and the friendships I’ve built here are ones I’ll always cherish. I can’t recommend this program enough for any newcomer woman looking to grow, connect, and feel empowered. Thank you NEW❤️

I participated in the workshops and in the Sister2Sister program, and the experience was truly transformative. Following my involvement with Newcomer Women, I was able to secure a meaningful position and begin my career in Canada. Their support was essential in achieving this milestone. I am sincerely grateful for the services they provided and would highly recommend their programs to others.

I truly enjoyed the class and learned a lot. I would also like to thank my teacher, Mehrshad, for being patient, supportive, and encouraging throughout the course. He not only helped us develop our language skills, but also guided us with practical knowledge about life in Canada, which gave us hope and motivation for building a better future here.
Thank you!

I had a meeting with Zuhal Najib, as a necomer i felt welcomed and encouraged. I really appreciate the efforts she put in to help and guide me, it made a huge difference in my perspective towards life in Canada. Thank you sooo much Zuhal.

I would like to express my sincere appreciation for the kindness and wonderful support provided by Zuhal Najib. Even though I was late for my appointment, she still assisted me with patience and warmth, providing all the necessary information, support, and time. I am truly grateful for the support received from Zuhal and NEW.

name: Oasis Addiction Recovery Society
address: 921 Danforth Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M4J 1L8
description:
The people that work here are literally angels. They helped me through my darkest days and always had kind words, advice, next steps and patience toward me; all while being completely real and non-judgemental. BEST. PEOPLE. EVER.

The team at Oasis including staff who have since moved on and are no longer with them have all helped me a great deal.   They  helped me to rediscover my confidence and gain the  necessary skills to seek out new career oppertunities.  I felt completely helpless  at one point but having a team of supportive people who actually care about helping me got me through that barrier.  The guys at Oasis have a genuine desire to help there clients succeed in there goals and are an excellent support group.  I am truly grateful for their help and ongoing support and amazed with the quality and care they put into their work.!!!!

A place that honours all souls, celebrates self improvement and supports ones journey with an underlying tone of grace, stability and strength. Many Thanks to all 🙏🏽🔆❤️

never have i ever met a place that actually cares and wants to see you aim all of your goals and more some until i landed the chance to be apart of this program they have to offer. the most amazing and beautiful people who work here. they've helped me during a very low time and i can't thank them enough for seeing something in me when i couldn't.

Great staff and empathetic energy all around in and out of this place. Thousand thumbs up!

name: ontrac Employment Resource Services
address: 52 Abbott Street N, Smiths Falls, Ontario K7A 1W3
description:
Efficient and professional.  I'm going back.

Ontrac lifts you up then drops you. They come up with reasons not to come through. Twice I have been asked if I  thought I would be capable of doing the course.
They have basically asked me if I thought I was smart enough.
Then because of my anxiety , (which by the way they made worse.) could I do an online course.  She didn't feel  that working from home would be exceptable for them to pay for.The best question was "when my doctor would think I would be ready to work."
Hello is ans

Been using the resource centre for a while now, staff is encouraging, kind and very helpful which is more than I can say for the receptionist. I recommend going in when she’s not there, other staff go out of their way to help people and make the environment positive, friendly and welcoming. The older receptionist is unapproachable, doesn’t offer any help and lacks sensitivity and listening skills. People go in to ask questions and she cuts them off before they can finish their question/story. If you need help on the computer from her, forget it. My first time going in I left feeling like I never wanted to go back, I’m glad I did, she wasn’t there and my experience was completely different. It’s a shame, what a great resource. Smiths Falls is a small community and no one should leave a place like that feeling not heard or supported. Unfortunately, my nephew has had a very similar experience with this receptionist. Customer Service training is needed.

One of the best employment services i have received. The staff over there are so helpful in all the ways..

Was really hoping to go there for help from someone to make a resume today because I haven't made one in years, instead they point me to a computer and told me to do it myself. Gee thanks if I could do it myself i would have done it myself at home. So I guess I will have to go somewhere else or ask someone to help me.

name: Operation Springboard
address: 3195 Sheppard Avenue East, Unit 1A, Toronto, Ontario M1T 3K1
description:
Very helpful and my fav place to drop by to get some work done out of all the centres I’ve been in in Toronto!! The people are very helpful and make sure you’re actually helped out to the best of your abilities, no lazy run arounds. 😄

ps. free wifi

Very pleased with the service, I got to talk to a great job counselor Chan. He goes the extra mile to help me with job options and answers within afew days. They actively help you unlike other places. Easy to walk in to book workshops, I like that there are computers to use.

They gave me very useful tips on finding a job. Mr Chan was also very helpful and receptive!.

Had a promising opportunity from this organization could have offered me, but turns out I got financially screwed over and my career got hurt. From being a client to an underpaid and unsupported digital media facilitator, I feel this org did not really help me with my employment situation and only made it worse when I signed up to them. My counselor Rusi was alright, but she was not really a help improving my LinkedIn at all which I should not have listened to and especially finding a solid job opportunity for you.  Time that I moved on from them and find a way to get my career back on track.

Just a heads up- When you get to the building, you need to go to the back parking lot for the entrance. Ring the doorbell for until 105 to access their services. They offer free personalized job search assistance to people who are eligible, but anyone can go in to use their computers, print, attend employment workshops, etc.

name: Peel Career Assessment Services – PCAS
address: 975 Meyerside Drive, Mississauga, Ontario L5T 1P9
description:
I am very grateful to Gulab Ahmadzai for the excellent service he provided to me and my family. He helped us throughout our procedures with professionalism, patience, and great attention to detail. He made sure everything was clearly explained and guided us step by step, which made the whole process much less stressful for us.
Gulab was always kind, responsive, and willing to go the extra mile to make sure we felt supported. Thanks to his help, everything went smoothly, and we felt confident that we were in good hands. I highly recommend Gulab Ahmadzai to anyone looking for reliable and outstanding service.

would like to sincerely thank Gulab Ahmadzai and Sonia for their excellent service. They have always helped us and treated us with great kindness and respect.

Their hard work, dedication, and effort are truly appreciated. We are very grateful for everything they have done for us. They always try their best to provide great service, and their work deserves recognition.

Thank you so much for your support and kindness. We are very happy and thankful for your service.

Mr. Gulab Ahmadzai has filed my taxes and has taken time to explain everything I needed to know.
He is the best with excellent customer service.
Thank you Peel Career Assessment Services.

Peel career assessment services Inc  is one the best organizations.
They helped me through my PR application with great efficiency and attention. The in-person reception is excellent with enough time and the remote meeting for follow up is perfect and accommodates people from far. I highly recommend Mr. Gulab and his colleagues for all references.

I am truly grateful to Gulab Ahmadzai for his outstanding support during my Canadian citizenship application. He guided me with great care, clarity, and attention to detail, which made the whole process feel smooth and stress-free. His dedication and genuine effort meant a lot to me, and I deeply appreciate all his help with this important milestone.

name: Performance Plus Rehabilitative Care Inc. – Ottawa (Head Office)
address: 150 Elgin Street, Ottawa, Ontario K2P 1L4
description:
They did not help me feel ready for a job and their other assistance felt like they wasted my time. I regret signing up with them despite having paid nothing to do so.

Performance Plus Rehabilitative Care Inc is hidden gem with great course to expand skills to develop towards a workplace.

I believe being patience with each team member is very important as they all instruments as team leading, directing, guiding each client to serve each one as best as they can with the tools they are given to provide each client.

I will keep expanding my skills with this company as I am very happy with what I have learned so far; I have moments with an consultant I choose to be direct and firm to connect with the team to receive what I am seeking to achieve the career goals.

I wish Performance Plus Rehabilitative Care Inc will receive renewed positive feedback in the future because I see the good, the potential, And I see area of improvement in this service for  who all seek this program to build the skills toward resilience for change for the business, the employees, and every client because each person is working hard towards their goals to achieve in success in many ways.

God bless.

I can't express enough gratitude for the incredible support I received from Performance Plus Rehabilitation Care Inc. (PPRC). They not only played a pivotal role in helping me to retain employment, but also went above and beyond by offering unwavering support, personalized skill training and constant encouragement. Their commitment to empowering individuals with disabilities extends beyond job placement - they have helped me to learn how to advocate for my rights including the right for reasonable accommodation so that I can sustainably participate in the workforce. Being able to participate in the workforce will directly lead to an increase in security and stability for my young family. They also taught me how to navigate the professional landscape with optimism, focus and confidence. I'm immensely thankful for their dedicated team and the positive impact they make. I would highly recommend this organization for their transformative approach, authenticity and genuine care!

It took some time to get my first appointment, but it was totally worth it. They helped me revamp my cv, create a solid cover letter, and find/apply for jobs. They helped me get a good job in the end. It wasn't instant, it depends what field your interested in. I got several different case workers over my time there and some were better than others, but most were very good. Don't expect to get a job on the first day and it can take several months to get the right job for you. That's just the nature of the job market. As I am sure you noticed, it is very hard to find good work these days. It can takes months before organizations even reply to you with an interview invite, and many organizations won't reply at all. You just have to be persistent. That's the reality of the modern job market. I stayed with them after I got a job with their help and eventually they connected me with a job in my field of interest.

Written Dec 2023

In Feb/April 2023 I came to realize that I was on the autism spectrum. And in May I began working with PPRC. What followed was a mix of incompetence, indifference and utter lack of empathy that has left me worse off than when I began.

Some notable issues include:
- An excessively long process to get to Job Development. Which after researching other providers, I have learned is atypical.
- Resumes that were incorrectly formatted or had spelling errors.
- No support from my job developer. Either in the form of leads or follow-ups
- Emails that were not replied to. Vital questions that were never answered.

Those are just the top-line examples I have many others. What made the experience so awful, was that I assumed PPRC and its management cared about helping disabled people find sustainable work. In all my time dealing with them I felt like a bother, an annoyance and that they did not care, whatsoever, about helping me or my situation.

I have no doubt, whatsoever that had I either worked with a different provider or on my own I would not be in the current situation I find myself in.

Avoid PPRC and find another provider. They are out there.

name: Pinecrest-Queensway Community Health Centre
address: 2529 Carling Avenue, 2nd floor, Ottawa, Ontario K2B 7H6
description:
I have been a client for 33 years. I have to say the current staff is the best so far. Ariel at the front desk is amazing, compassionate and goes the extra mile to make sure you get what you need done. I would give her 5 stars too if I could.

Thought I’d pop by and leave a review for the new parents that are bringing their baby in for their newborn hearing assessment as the rest of the reviews aren’t that positive and they may cause some anxiety. My husband was working so I brought the baby in solo! We had our appointment today at 11:30 with Jana. The receptionist was very friendly and directed us to the waiting area upon our arrival. Jana was amazing ! She was very thorough and gave me all the information I needed. She took extra time with my son as he was breathing loudly and was a little fussy. Rest assured that she will do everything she can to complete this test so you don’t have to rebook. I know there is so much pressure to keep your baby asleep, and general anxiety about the test/ results. You can only do so much as a parent. I hope this review helps at least someone put their mind at ease :)

Ive been getting alot of help from Madison Rodney, she has been amazing with finding me solutions for fitting in the right job feild using nessasary resources to help guide me to where I need to be in the proper workplace. Five Stars for Madison Rodney because she is super awsome and so caring. And Jasmine at the front counter is so cool to help schedule my appointments on days that best work for me, you couldn't ask for a better pair of Fantastic helpers.

The receptionist was extremely rude and unhelpful, it felt like the last thing was wanted to do was help people entering the community center! Don’t bother speaking to her for help if you come here, you’re better off finding your way on your own.

About a month ago I was waiting in their reception when I young black mom came in and asked a question in French.

I came here for counseling service 2 years ago, and my counselor was amazing. Later on I would like to connect with my counselor, I heard that she left her job and went somewhere else.

I felt bad about seeing the negative review here since the staff here has been eager to help out clients.

However, I heard from a friend who was a volunteer here about this place. She told me to stay away from here if you would like to be a staff or volunteer since it is not a good environment to work there. They really need a new leadership or the staff turnover rate is very high. Nevertheless, the staff here are very friendly and professional. For staff, I would give 5 stars. I took away 2 stars due to some other issues in this agency.

I recently observed an employee of this centre threatening the life of a person who didn’t want to wear a pride jersey for an NHL warmup game. The employees response was “someone with some spare ammunition should empty off a few shells into this corpse”.

I reported it to the centre as that mentality is clearly unsafe especially when dealing with persons who may have been subject to violence. The centres response was that they thought it was perfectly acceptable and applauded the employee for saying it.

Very supportive family center for immigrants especially women in need.

Women Center.

name: RNC Employment Services
address: 4151 Sheppard Avenue East, Main Floor, Scarborough, Ontario M1S 1T4
description:
I had a great experience with RNC Employment Services. Participating in their activities and programs helped me improve my communication skills and build confidence. I learned both soft skills and hard skills, made new friends, and gained valuable experience and understanding of the Canadian job market.
I would especially like to thank Shannon Aycenk for her amazing support and guidance. She helped me a lot throughout the process and was always kind, patient, and encouraging. The staff truly care about helping clients succeed. I highly recommend RNC Employment Services to anyone looking to grow professionally and gain real-world experience.  Thank you RNC Employment Services.

This review captures the core strengths of the RNC program.
What I liked:
- Excellent Concept & Vision: The foundational idea of the program is powerful and relevant.
- Inspiring Introduction: Samantha Maxwell was phenomenal. Her passion and personal alignment with the program's mission were  not only convincing but genuinely inspiring. She was the perfect ambassador.
- Quality Content: The workshops were well-structured, informative, and delivered clear educational value.

What Could be Improved:
- Participant Communication: This was the weakest part of my experience. The communication from the operational staff was often unclear, slow, or ineffective, which created unnecessary friction.

Recommendation:
- The program has a clear opportunity to elevate the participant experience by leveraging its greatest asset: Samantha Maxwell. I strongly recommend integrating Samantha into the participant communication loop. Having someone who embodies the program's spirit and vision as a point of contact would ensure the inspirational start she provides continues throughout the program, making the entire experience more cohesive and supportive.

Rnc employment is an amazing employment service and are very welcoming patient positive and helpful. They helped me get back on my feet, with jobs, organizations and preparing for interviews, exams and certifications. Especially Adam Bulley and his team they supported me a lot with tools and equipments trainings needed and to not give up when times are hard especially with starting to build a career. I’m forever grateful for Rnc employment services.

Enjoyed learning Health & Safety Awareness at RNC 4151 Sheppard Ave, this is a invaluable resource centre for everyone.  Adam of RNC is passionate in empowering all workers and in so building stronger communities.  Kudos!!!

I am incredibly grateful for the outstanding support I received from Rehibition Network Canada Employment Services (RNC). From the moment I connected with their team, I was met with professionalism, warmth, and a genuine commitment to helping me navigate my employment journey.

A special thank you to my kind-hearted facilitator, Marcia Murray, who was exceptionally accommodating, knowledgeable, and always willing to go the extra mile to provide guidance and resources tailored to my needs. The assistance I received in resume building, job search strategies, and interview preparation was invaluable. Thanks to their support, I gained more confidence in my job search and felt empowered to pursue opportunities that align with my skills and experience.

Additionally, I had the privilege of benefiting from several courses through the Advanced Leadership Program offered by Newcomer Women’s Services Toronto. These courses helped me develop essential leadership skills, improve my communication, and enhance my overall professional growth. The knowledge and insights I gained from these programs will be instrumental in shaping my career path in Canada.

I highly recommend RNC to anyone seeking employment support. Their dedication to empowering individuals is truly commendable. Thank you to the entire team for making such a positive impact on my career journey!

— Phina Asika

name: Street Haven
address: 87 Pembroke Street, Toronto, Ontario M5A 2N9
description:
Spoke to someone called Nicola/Michelle. She was very rude. Lied that I called her three times, previously. I’ve never called a shelter before then, it was actually my first time. I followed up to call a second time to inform someone in charge that the way the place is being run isn’t providing safe space for women, the person refused to get a manager and tried to have a fight with me. This call was for a friend who asked me to call on her behalf, as she wanted to make sure the staff was friendly and the place was safe space. The person that answered is wicked and needs to be replaced if she hates women in need that badly. She was rude, snappy and hung up instead of helping look for a solution.

Overall a very good shelter.  Staff member Nicola is particularly pro-active and professional. Whenever I have mentioned a need, she gets going on it immediately, whether it's to accompany me outside when I felt threatened by someone, or to get on the phone and find answers to my questions, she respects every woman guest and treats us as adults. Meals are sadly missing sufficient fruits and vegetables and complex carbohydrates such as beans and healthy legumes, however.  Otherwise safe and basic needs all met.

I had a traumatic experience and ran away from home.  This Shelter gave me the rest and support I needed.  Nicola is so loving and caring.  Her commitment is amazing.  The director Bobbie made Christmas dinner for all the Residents herself.  Every dish was excellent.  I was lucky to be there for Christmas.  All the Residents were given many gifts and brand new beautiful cozy pyjamas.  They put a smile on all our faces.

I can see why Misha works there. I can also see why she wouldn't be successful working anywhere else. I also feel the same about the other lady.. homeless shelter workers are almost always worse than the people that stay there.

The best place ever. Supportive people. Very home, and welcoming with lots of great experiences. I can say. Most of the girls there are trauma experienced sad, hurt. Everyone has their own experiences for the most. I didn’t like my room as at night the girl across from my bunk bed would put music on when we tried to sleep. The other woman, would wrap her marijuana in the room (I kind of found that odd really) I told the staff. One girl was laughing and she got up and did some weird stuff and the other lady got up and was disrupted and upset. These experiences are very scary. Make sure your in a safe place.

name: The Career Foundation
address: 2150 Islington Avenue, Suite 210, Etobicoke, Ontario M9P 3V4
description:
I had a very positive and rewarding experience with The Career Foundation, and I would like to sincerely thank the entire team for their support and dedication.

Throughout the Networking / Network Cabling program, the staff at Career Foundation were incredibly supportive, professional, and well organized. The program was not only technically strong and practical, but it also provided real exposure to the Canadian job market.

Thanks to Career Foundation, we had the opportunity to attend the CANASA – Security Canada Central Expo in Toronto, which was an excellent experience for networking, learning about industry trends, and connecting with employers. In addition, completing our training in collaboration with partner organizations and having access to high-quality online learning sessions throughout the program added significant value to our professional development.

Career Foundation truly stands out for its commitment to helping newcomers and job seekers succeed. Their guidance, industry connections, and continuous support made a meaningful difference in our career journey.

I am very grateful for everything the Career Foundation team has done and would confidently recommend this organization to anyone looking to build or advance their career in Canada.

The Career Foundation is a wonderful resource and career development agency helping so many immigrants (and non immigrants) navigate different career bridging programs here in Canada.
I am particularly impressed with their approach to take each candidate based on their respective needs and work with them from start to finish (trainings, certifications and jobs offers).
Of the so many job development and support agencies across Ontario and Canada, this foundation does not only bring you into their well thought processes, they are there with you, checking in timely to make sure that the results are effective and get you the desired results of securing a job.

A high recommendation for any new immigrant especially skilled immigrant who find it hard to navigate the employment market. Here is a place you will obtain all the necessary tools to optimize and most your chances and career. Kudos to the team members, the program managers and the sponsors.

Love from Jade

I would like to share my great experience with the Network Cabling program at The Career Foundation. The program was not only technical but also included interview preparation, where we learned how to present ourselves professionally and answer behavioral interview questions.

Even after the program ended, the organization continued to support us by assigning each participant an employment advisor to help with the job search.

I would especially like to thank Kristoffer for his guidance in helping me finalize my resume and prepare for interviews. His advice played a big role in helping me land an interview for a Data Center Operations Technician role at Amazon, which I’m happy to say I successfully secured. I truly believe I would not have gotten this opportunity without his support

I sincerely appreciate everyone at The Career Foundation for this wonderful opportunity and their continued support.

I had the pleasure of having Amanda as my facilitator in the ReSet Program at The Career Foundation, and I am truly grateful for her guidance and support.

Amanda is an exceptional facilitator who creates a positive, motivating, and inclusive learning environment. She explains concepts clearly, provides practical career advice, and always encourages participants to believe in their potential. Her professionalism, patience, and dedication made a significant impact on my confidence and job readiness.

Through the ReSet Program, I gained valuable knowledge about resume building, interview preparation, and workplace expectations, and Amanda was always ready to answer questions and provide personalized feedback. She genuinely cares about participants’ success and goes above and beyond to ensure everyone feels supported.

I highly recommend Amanda as a facilitator. She is knowledgeable, inspiring, and truly committed to helping individuals achieve their career goals.

I would like to express my sincere gratitude to Career Foundation for their outstanding support throughout my integration process in Canada.

Thanks to their guidance, I was able to become certified in my dream career: network cabling, a major milestone in my professional journey in Canada.

I would like to warmly thank the entire team, and especially:

Bushra Kotan and Emily Armstrong, for delivering a highly professional one-week training on the Canadian workplace in Ontario. This training was extremely valuable in helping me understand workplace culture and employer expectations in Canada.

Ann Wu, for her personalized guidance, attentive support, and for directing me toward the network cabling program, which perfectly matched my career goals.

Sahra Warsame and Sandra Velasquez, who ensured my successful enrollment in the program and provided meticulous follow-up until completion. They go above and beyond to help ensure we are successfully recruited by companies in the network cabling sector.

I am truly grateful for their well-organized services, dedication, and strong commitment to the socio-professional integration of immigrants.

👉 I highly recommend Career Foundation to anyone looking for serious, structured, and human-centered support to build a professional future in Canada.

An encourage me to keep applying anywhere I could AN KEPT it going, I remember when I did well at the Canadian Tire interview but I never received a phone call, I was a bit distraught, shocked but she encouraged me that IT may not be the job for me or God's plans fast forward a month or two later, Pearson Airport was hiring and there was a big hiring job fair that she told me to attend she had everything I would need including my social which I didn't know on my HEAD. anyway I went and aced the interview and now Im a walking proud man from the months and months looking and seeking jobs I'm very thankful for Melissa for helping me along the way and I just wanted to write this to show my gratitude and appreciation  tho a lengthy one just to say... GOD.. BLESS YOU!... KEEP Changing lives!
