....

name: Office of Inclusive Postsecondary Education (OIPE), University of Missouri–St. Louis
address: 113 Lucas Hall, 1 University Boulevard, St. Louis, MO 63121-4400
description:
I visited this campus in Fall of 2025 to assist my daughter with moving in and settling on dorm. She had chosen UMSL as her place to shine and prepare for the next phase of life. This selection was made from a pool of over 20 universities all covering varying degrees of scholarships. UMSL checked all our boxes and was not the least expensive, however our daughter's and our peace of mind was not to be compromised. Communication with her Recruiter/Advisor, Matheus was seamless leading up to and after making the choice.  Matheus even visited our island as part of his recruitment strategy and it was in one of those sessions my daughter's was enagegd. Discussions with other members of staff were warm and cordial leading up to and after arriving at UMSL.Dan in the Financial Services Office at the MSC with whom I spoke several times provided valuable information. Dr. Stallings who is my daughter's Academic Advisor was lovely in person as she was on the phone and Dr. Jordan who attended to us in her absence on 1 occasion when we got to UMSL for Orientation week designated herself as my daughter's on campus Mom😊 Dr. Jessica was full of energy and vibrant and met the students where they were. On to campus...it's clean with lots of green spaces which I absolutely love. Tgere a couple geese and squirrels too! At the time we got there, construction was ongoing however you literally can see and feel the capital expenditure and reinvestment in the university. There were charging and sanitization stations throughout the MSC where all Orientation activities were based. The campus is huge and has 2 train stations (North and South campus) and a police post on site. The Nosh has a couple options for dining and seating appears adequate as it can get busy. The dorms are consistent with normal dorm size and are equipped with bed, study table, closet, dresser, microwave and fridge plus retractable blinds. You do not share dorm room (which was HIGH on our list of requirements) but bathroom is shared among 4 suite mates who are responsible for cleaning. Freshmen must reside on Oak Hall if living on campus and it is has a kitchen, wash room, pool area, game room, garbage disposal etc. Can you tell yet? We loved our selection and generally have been satisfied with our daughter's settling so far. She has confirmed that she too loves her university and campus. Very satisfied Mommy...go Tritons!!🧡💛 If you're ever scouting for universities in the US, check UMSL out, they're highly recommended!

P.S. It's rated in the top 5 for safest campus in the US and commute was fairly easy via metro or UBER when I visited over the 12 days. We also had no incidents of theft, violence or crime and the people are warm and friendly....pretty laid back.

Let me just preface this by the fact that I am not actually a student here yet, but not for lack of trying on my part. I started this application process in January and it is now March and I still do not have an admissions decision, even though I have done everything they have asked me to and more. I’ve reached out multiple times to my admissions specialist without a response. There seemed to be some confusion between my maiden name and my married name which I made sure to point out to them from the get-go, but it still caused a ton of issues.  When I reached out to UMSL via Facebook messenger because I wanted to see if there was someone else I could work with, I was told that my admission specialist had been sick and that she would get a hold of me.
It is very frustrating to send continuous emails and have some not be responded to, but at the same time still getting the automated emails that say “we still need your transcripts.” I submitted my additional transcripts on 2/18 and was told that they would be updated the next day, yet I still continued to get automated emails stating they needed my transcripts.As a non-traditional student waiting over two months for an acceptance decision when another local university responded to me within a week and sent me my acceptance letter, school email, online log in, etc. coupled with the severe lack of communication, tells me about all I need to know about attending this school.

My child transferred from SIUE to UMSL this semester; tuition/fees is 42% higher; “automatic scholarships” are much more restrictive—can only be used for tuition not other university expenses and definitely not refunded; Financial Aid awarded a scholarship then revoked it & charged us a late fee & interest because they mistakenly awarded aid. They don’t care about the students financial wellbeing…consistently encouraging him to get a loan he doesn’t need. Overall, this was the worse decision he could have made.

UMSL doesn't like unconventional students. If you are taking classes at two schools at once, (which working adults often have to), UMSL refuses to sign a  consortium agreement. They give the excuse that if they offer the class, you should take it with them.  In my case that would mean delaying my graduation an extra year!

Now let's discuss financial aid. Now for a state school UMSL is a little higher than some of the surrounding states, but they'll give you a merit scholarship if you have a high GPA (which I did). Your financial aid page in myview will show that you are awarded the scholarship before classes begin. Then 7-14 days later, the financial aid office will tell you that they are revoking it, because you are a part time student (even if a consortium agreement would prove otherwise; see what they do? :) ).

But wait! It's only 10 days into the semester so you'll have to pay for half of your classes, it's their drop policy. So they hook you with the promise of scholarship for being a top student, and then they pull the rug out once it's too late to avoid paying for classes. Very classy.

So they don't offer classes when you need them, get uncooperative when you do what's best for your education, and if you are a working adult taking a healthy course load and still maintaining a 3.8 GPA, they will not give you a scholarship for this excellence.

Do yourself a favor and walk around the campus. Ask yourself, is this school thriving or dying? UMSL knows the answer. They've tried nothing to solve it, and they're all out of ideas.

Attending University Of Missouri St. Louis is financial money pit designed to drain your wallet and to fill your life with student loans that you can never pay off. When I attended they refused to honor half of my 64 credited courses that I passed at another credited university in the area. For the degree that I was completing I was required to participate in an internship program on campus despite the degree being offered one hundred percent online. Missouri Has outdated educational requirements requiring certain " core" classes to graduate. UMSL would not accept one of those core classes due to a low but passing grade.

Financial aid and student support services Is a joke. You don't get financial aid assistance unless you are a full time student and what money you do get is based off of income. In today's society with the high rising cost of living it simply isn't worth attending collage and even if you do it doesn't guarantee you a Job.

name: Watton Employment Services Inc.
address: 9 Elgin Street East, Suite 7
description:
I just finished four weeks of the Youth Employment and Skills Strategy (YESS) program. This program, and all the staff I got to meet at Watton, were excellent. I came into Watton unsure of myself and my goals for employment- but with the support from the friendly staff here, I’ve been able to develop my skills and strengths that I can use in employment AND every day life. I’m thrilled with the support and services provided at Watton.

I went to Watton's Employment Services back in Feb 2025. I was going to update my Resume and was going to apply for jobs as I was new in the area. When I arrived I began to get a little frustrated as to being able to format my Resume in such a way to fit all my info. I left Watton's and decided to call for a S.O.S.
From that point I was put in touch with Janet Desjardins a worker at Watton's. She not only helped me get in touch with the right people to help with my Resume and Cover letter but also signed me up for a job fair. I attended the job fair and landed a job where I am working to this day.
I appreciate you Janet Desjardins for the help and the future you have helped me to obtain.
Thank-you Watton's

This place saved my life! I was underemployed, struggling to make ends meet and couldn't get a job despite how many resumes I filled out. Janet Desjardins was my case manager and she gave me so many leads from potential jobs to potential agencies that could help! Cathy Cope lead all the workshops I took about how to write a resume, a cover letter and how to present at an interview. She took the time to look over my resume and cover letter and give me great suggestions. I hope I never have to look for a job again because this is the one I want but if I do, this is the first place I'm going! Thank you Watton!

I'd been trying to find remote work for almost a year before I reached out to Watton. It was difficult to find something that would fit my exact situation and skills, but eventually they found the perfect place for me! They really fought hard to give me a huge opportunity, and now I hope it will be my life-long career. I highly recommend anyone looking for work in the area to give them a call.

An amazing establishment that has amazing resources and knowledge sharing to help or improve employment opportunities. Excellent and compassionate staff that are always there to guide, listen and help. I would definitely recommend for anymore struggling to find employment, needing resumè updating/tweeking, interview assistance, certificates/training or would love to explore new employment possibilities in Northumberland.

name: Publications Ontario
address: 777 Bay Street, Toronto, Ontario
description:
A lil late in writing this review, but here I go. This review in particular is for a person named Tracy counter #15. Life had been hard for me for awhile and to encounter her, had made my year! (no joke) She showed such gentleness and kindness which I'll never forget. Without going into too much detail, I needed help with my health card and drivers license. It was a bit complicated and she made the whole process easy and seamless. I'm so very thankful that she was so kind, that I had cried quite a bit behind closed doors. I'm forever grateful to you Tracy! Management please read this, she does her job extremely well and deserves recognition of some sort. I, myself have worked in retail management for over 30 years. So I know excellent customer service when I receive it! Give her an award, raise, praise...something! Hopefully next time I visit, I'll be lucky enough to be sent to you Tracy. I wish you all the best!

It's decently sized and has 22 service points, so you don't need to worry about spending an eternity in queue. We went there for an OHIP card and finished everything in less than 30 minutes with about 40 people in queue.

Very fast service even for walking and no appointment, u can still get in and leave in max half an hour , they have like 40-50 counters so they call numbers.  Very quickly but if u want u can always make an appointment online just to be safe but overall friendly service and very quick

Lethargic staff.
Extremely long waiting times.
Irregular ticket call to windows, even for the same service.
No advance appointment booking available at any day/time for over a month.

Just had my 3rd coversation with Service Ontario regarding the issuance of the Green Book for Construction with amendment inserts for complete legislation up to 2022.

1st representative said this publication was available with inserts for $16.00

2nd representative said we can't sell you the amendment inserts with the January 2020 book without having been notified by Occupational Health & Safety that they are available (he said there was not notification within depts that these inserts were ready) so I asked him to put me through to Occupational Health & Safety.

Final representative at Occupational Health & Safety didn't understand what I was asking so we took a bit more time straightening that out which I was happy to do for our mutual benefit.   When she understood what I required, she said the January  2020 edition with amendment inserts was available now fat the cost of $8.00...as I tried to reconcile this half price, she unfortunately displayed an unprofessional manner like I was getting on her last nerve.    I told not to be irritated because I just had to have the correct information (for my report to the prospective purchaser of several copies).   I still don't get why all three people had completely different information.

I don't believe any of them care if we make the purchase with them or not...they still get their pay cheque and their superiors wouldn't have a clue that all three gave me different answers to the same question.   Does anyone have to answer for this apparent pandemonium at Service  Ontario or do the taxpayers (who pay their salary) just have to accept this quality of service?

name: Ontario Human Rights Commission
address: 180 Dundas Street West, 7th floor, Toronto, Ontario M7A 2R9
description:
Very rude phone operator to my son who is special needs. I couldn't believe they didn't help just don't want to do their jobs. He ended up hanging the phone up from the rude lady.

I mean, all one needs to understand exactly what this organization actually is to listen to the 7 minute long process before they put you on hold for 1+ hour ( at best) and count the number of times they tell you are either 1. in the wrong place 2. what they will not help you with 3. how you must behave 4. are you sure you're in the right place should I continue. Well done OHRC well done.

OHRC has refused to investigate my complaints regarding discrimination and unlawful emailed threats against me by Furnal Equinox, a convention in Toronto. There really is no point in filing these sorts of complaints with them because they'll just throw you under the bus without offering real solutions.

I was refused entry into a bookstore despite me declaring my medical exemption status to not wear a mask. I complained to a Human rights rep, who had the gull to tell me that since the store was offering me curbside pick up, they were not refusing service..they were refusing to give me an accommodation. and that the curbside pick up was the accommodation.
and that if a store refuses to serve you outside then you call us back.

called back months later after a popeyes chicken refused to serve me outside. OHRC rep told me that I failed to provide proof that I have an exemption so they did not have to serve me period.

Unlike Public Health and By-Law which claim proof is not required. OHRC believes that you have to show proof of a medical condition, otherwise anybody could just say they are exempt, but will allow biological men to go into women's spaces like locker rooms.. if they just declare they identify as females. OHRC is so woke they can give a bottle of Ambien a sleepless night.

It's a scam!!! First thing out of the chair person ( aka judge) was " Full disclosure, I did not read any of the documents". After only my testimony she had her mind made up.. no name calling no case. You would think someone in that position would know discrimination is not limited to name calling. I paid over $30,000.00 of lawyer fee and this "judge" couldn't be bothered to read the documents that OHRC had me on tight deadlines to produce. Minorities are at the mercy of the majority, if they allow us to have rights that fine but thinking we have enforceable rights is a illusion.

They are absolutely amazing with the help they offer, assisted me on getting any needs that were required for work wear and shoes and or boots depending what the need is for. I encourage anyone to give them a chance to help you.. I'll say this in capitals.... UTILIZE YOUR RESOURCES

Amazing connections and resources to really get you back on your feet! Renee and Carina made the experience feel very calm and I walked out feeling much more confident in my chances of getting the right job for me!

Not very good experience with these organization especially if you are BLACK .their appointments never ends and they favors other , until when you are frustrated then you will give up , after you start finding solutions by yourself  they will show up to claim accomplishment .

The Sault Community Career Centre supported me on my journey as a newcomer to Sault Ste. Marie. When I first arrived, I had no idea how to navigate life here. It was a challenge finding work, settling in, or even knowing where to start. But this Centre became a lifeline for me.

Through their settlement services, I was guided through everything from understanding local systems to finding and accessing essential community resources. They made the transition so much less overwhelming.

Then came the employment services, which were just as impactful. The team helped me identify my strengths, update my resume, prepare for interviews, and understand the local job market. No small task in such a competitive environment.

Every person I interacted with was compassionate, professional, and genuinely committed to helping people succeed. This organization does not just offer services. They bring hope, direction, and the kind of support that changes lives.

If you are a newcomer, a job seeker, or simply looking for guidance in the Sault area, I strongly encourage you to reach out to the Sault Community Career Centre. Their support made all the difference for me and I know it can for others as well.

description:

I called and the front desk said "right across the road is where guests park, the workers park in the paid lot" I go into the lot she describes and all I see in there are vehicles of workers, all of which had parking passes and all parked in the Guest Parking lot. Had to park down the road a couple blocks in a free lot because the lot nearby was a paid lot and the ticket reader was walking through. Why are workers parked in the guest lot? It was totally fall. I'm sure he's a great teacher of coding and I'll find out next semester but bazlur Rasheed should be fired and the dean of the college hasn't done anything about it. Luckily we have a new Dean. You will be harassed, belittled and threatened from random facility simply because they do not like the way you present yourself (even when it's within student conduct standards) The academic dishonest is only taken seriously for candian students any preformed from international students will be overlooked even with proof because all the college cares about is money. There are a few good teachers but most simply won't do their jobs and any reports against teachers for harassment or not doing their jobs is not taken seriously. The non teaching faculty is also a joke as they will lie to you about what needed to be done to say enrolled (OSAP) so they don't have to do work then make you pay late fees after you were told not to worry about anything. This happened to several students so it's clear it's a money scam. If you are a Canadian student save your money and don't bother as you will be treated like garbage in favor of international students and most of the teachers do not care about student success or learning as they get paid regardless of if they do their jobs or not. It's a waste of time and money for candian students.

As an international student, we hear what sets Canadian apart is their kindness and empathy. I wonder if that is true or just internet propaganda. My experience and all of my friend’s experiences with this school has been nothing short of traumatizing. I will tell a story of my friend, who needed just a copy of her transcript to apply for her study permit extension and COOP work permit. She reached out to the school and the person that responded to her email was so mean and rude

She/he does not seem to care about her individual situation, all responses to her lengthy emails was generic - No, we cannot help you!  This was for a semester she already completed and paid for, which should be right to have access to her academic transcript.

Eventually, she had to run around and source for the funds applied for her COOP late. Due to long processing time from IRCC, her application is delayed, she cannot start COOP and now on the brink of losing her status. All because a school cannot show an iota of empathy and grant her temporary access to her transcript.

That’s just one friends experience, I can’t even begin to talk about mine or other friends’. The bottom line is this school is a money grabbing venture. Not a single soul is passionate about students or their success. They only want to line their pockets. Forgive any typo

Amazing staff, inclusive environment. You will receive support and be involved with many different cultures. Making many friends and achieving your goals never was so easy.

name: New Beginnings (The Detroit Medical Center)
address: 3901 Beaubien, Suite 1K-52
description:
I cannot say enough amazing things about the ER staff and surgical team who took care of my son during his emergency appendectomy.

After being told at another hospital that he did not have appendicitis, we were still uneasy because we knew something wasn’t right. From the moment we arrived here, the ER staff truly listened. They took his pain seriously, acted quickly, and ran the appropriate tests without dismissing our concerns.

Within a short time, they confirmed it was appendicitis — and the surgical team moved fast.

The surgeons, nurses, anesthesiology team, and support staff were absolutely incredible. They were calm, confident, compassionate, and thorough. They explained everything clearly, answered every question, and treated my son with such kindness and care. As a parent, that reassurance means everything.

His surgery went smoothly, and the follow-up care was just as attentive. We felt supported every step of the way.

Thank you for trusting your expertise, for listening to parents, and for literally saving my child from what could have become a very serious situation. We are beyond grateful.

Forever thankful. 💙

I cannot say enough positive things about the Fifth Floor at DMC/Childrens hospital of Michigan. The level of attentiveness, compassion, and urgency shown by the entire staff was truly exceptional.

My daughter was experiencing a very puzzling and complex medical situation, and thanks to Dr. Cares, who coordinated a direct admit for us and is affiliated with Children’s DMC/ Childrens, we were able to get immediate and appropriate care without delay. From the moment we arrived on the Fifth Floor, the team took swift, decisive action. They listened carefully, assessed thoroughly, and never dismissed our concerns.

The contrast between the care we received here and our experience at another pediatric hospital was night and day. At Children’s hospital of Michigan we felt heard, supported, and confident that our child’s well-being was the top priority.

As someone who works in healthcare management, I know how challenging it is to consistently deliver this level of care, communication, and teamwork, especially in today’s healthcare environment. That is exactly why I was so impressed. The Fifth Floor staff demonstrated professionalism, empathy, and true clinical awareness throughout our stay.

The Fifth Floor team and Dr. Cares helped turn an incredibly stressful situation into one where we felt reassured and supported every step of the way. I am extremely grateful for the care my daughter received and would not hesitate to recommend DMC/ Childrens hospital of Michigan to any family in need. HIGHLY, HIGHLY, RECOMMEND!!!!!

My recent stay at this hospital was incredibly disappointing and honestly unacceptable.

The room was visibly dirty, and we were required to share a bathroom, which did not feel sanitary — especially for pediatric patients. For over four hours after admission, no doctor came to see my son(2mo). I eventually had to leave the room and search for someone myself to get an update on his care.

What is most concerning, however, is the inconsistency in communication and documentation. I was strongly advised not to leave because my son required 0.5–1L of oxygen and was told it would be unsafe to discharge him. Yet I later learned that insurance was informed he did not require oxygen and was not ill enough to justify admission — resulting in my stay not being covered. The physician who reportedly communicated this to insurance never treated or assessed my son directly. His care was handled by a different provider.

The lack of coordination, cleanliness, and accountability made an already frightening situation significantly worse.

The only bright spot in our experience was Nurse Cassie. She was attentive, compassionate, and truly supportive during a very stressful time. She is the kind of nurse every family hopes to have.

I sincerely hope this hospital reevaluates its communication practices and patient care standards so other families do not have a similar experience.

Dr. Gonik and the entire team at Children’s Hospital gave our family an experience we will never forget. My daughter recently had a 10-hour surgery to receive cochlear implants on both sides, and from start to finish we felt truly supported.

Dr. Gonik was incredibly informative, patient, and reassuring throughout the entire process. During the surgery, the nurses and team contacted us every single hour with updates, which meant more to us than words can express. The anesthesiologists were amazing and took the time to answer all of our questions so we felt at ease.

A special thank you to nurses Diana and Anna, and the rest of the wonderful nursing staff who cared for our daughter with so much compassion. Shoutout to lead nurse Jamie, who personally came in before discharge to make sure everything went well. That level of care and attention made a huge difference for our family.

We are beyond grateful for such an incredible experience and a team that truly cares. We’re looking forward to watching our daughter continue to heal and hear before her 4th birthday 🥹💛

During my stay at Children’s Hospital I was very well taken care of by all the nurses and staff made sure all my needs were consistently met and understood. One nurse that stood out specifically was Pilar, she was very kind, personable, and understanding. She truly has a talent for taking care of her patients and helping them feel normal and safe, especially in a hospital. Not only did she work to comfort me but she stayed on top of my routine to ensure I stayed comfortable and medicated at all times. She made sure to advocate for me and my needs, I knew I was well taken care of in her hands. She set a plan for me every night, stuck to it, and helped me get a full night of  comfortable sleep. Shout out Pilar! She deserves it :)

I'll  be posting every day to some one contacts me in regards of me being on the verge of losing my housing voucher  as well as trying to leave me with back rent due to her not completing her work.

No long wait time. Reception was very nice.

I have had a very bad experience with this housing authority! Im in the process of porting my voucher to phoenix, and my previous voucher was for a 3 bedroom so I thought I would be granted what I already had and started looking for a 3 bedroom. Well their portability specialist Alice took a while getting back to me about my briefing and my new voucher, and when she finally did she gave me a voucher for a 2 bed room and I had a 3 bedroom (and I had found a place for what my previous voucher was for) so called and called and emailed for me to never get any response back from her. I then called and spoke with an operator who got me in touch with Alice, when she got on the phone she had the most rudest attitude you could ever imagine. She talked to me as if I was a piece of trash on the floor and then she hung up on me as I was talking. So I called back and spoke with the supervisor who was fairly kind of understanding but still didn’t fix the situation!!!! I don’t have anyone here to call on in these situations… and I expected them to be more understanding& they weren’t! So I am going to be porting back out of phoenix the first chance I get! I treat people with respect and want the same back in return. I was nothing but nice to them and they can be really rude to people who need help!

Ridiculous office to deal with they never answer the phone. They will send you a letter demanding more information and won't even tell you what information. Am I just supposed to provide random information to the housing department and hope that's what was requested. This office is operated by tax dollars. The people operating it are public servants and should serve the public and not make seniors jump through hoops just to find affordable housing. Absolute trash experience dealing with this organization maybe if you identify as an illegal immigrant they would give you same day express housing.

name: Grand Forks Housing Authority (Family Self Sufficiency Coordinator: Craig Knudsvig)
address: 1405 1st Avenue North, Grand Forks, ND 58203
description:
To anyone who is trying to get on this assistance program or voucher it is best to not do it sadly because these people do not answer the phone at all. They send you to voicemail every single time then the customer service on top of that is so beyond unprofessional. Sees like they have an attitude with you just for calling. Nor do they return phone call. This has to be the best job ever I’m assuming. I don’t even understand how they have a job. . Treating people this way, And it seems like it’s all about who you know because I’ve been on this waitlist for over eight months almost a year. I literally call monthly and I still get no type of help. So if you want to be frustrated or overwhelmed with trying to get help or resources come here. Due to it, not being a lot of people in this town it’s still a very long waiting process.

It seems to be general Housing Authority policy to dodge calls and blame you for it. I've had extreme problems with Siva in prior years and this month the receptionist has gone straight to voicemail four times and not responded to any of them. I finally got a hold of her to schedule my Housing Quality Inspection (Without which I will be homeless in 2 weeks) and she had the gall to talk down to me about how late in the month I had waited to schedule. I'm not even asking her to say something nice or be immediately available. Just get back to me eventually and don't call me "boy" like I'm a child laborer in your 1900s factory.

Eh, when I went there one of the receptionists was very abrasive and condescending. She was very cold when I asked her for some general information pertaining to the qualification standards and dismissed me once I asked.

Instead of having any substance to a conversation, I was left with aversive and dodgy manners. It was extremely difficult to have a conversation that I could extract any useful information without being deferred or told to wait for questions that were elementary (as she conveyed to me).

My case worker 'forgot' to send in my verification for a voucher which put a damper on the process and put be in a worse-off position in line. It was alarming to say the least because I was planning on quick approval because I met all the qualifications.

Obviously, there is not a substitute in town for the service they offer, but that doesn't mean that they should be allowed to treat the people who come in as low-life and degenerates.

When I think of Grand Forks Housing Authority, the first thing that comes to mind is communication and how poorly they are at it.  It seems close to impossible to ever get ahold of anyone at all. They never answer the phone, and also it takes them, what seems like an eternity, to respond to an email.  Never go through them.

Honestly I loved the place, I loved the landlord and the neighbours. BUT. We had just been evicted as of today, the property manager claims we didn't pay . they took our check, cashed it, then returned it. I have recipts proving I paid and they just dissmised me. If yours looking for a place to live I would strongly suggest looking elsewhere. Unless you want to end up homeless and out of money.

address: 120 6th Avenue North
description:
I wish I could give negative 5 stars.  I've had Section 8 tenants for many years.  My direct phone calls to the responsible agent and his manager are never answered.  I have left many voicemails that are never returned.  Not once ever.  I have even mailed a letter which also go unanswered.  It's like a blackhole that sucks everything in but nothing ever comes out.
The only time I get a response is when I make an appointment to see a live person. The last time I was there, I had to wait 15 minutes into my 30 minute appointment before someone came out.  During that time, about 7 young employees came to the front desk to bullshitting about their personal life at 8:30 am.  They were not the front desk clerks as the front desk clerks were sitting there doing nothing but checking on their phones.

I believe Seattle housing authority they being able to provide for most low income housing assistance it's very difficult operation it's a lot of work and takes a lot of energy to make for everyone makes happy in safe and they are trying to shelter for everyone

Super friendly and helpful staff. No not perfect but every question, concern or issue I had was resolved. Yes they could be faster but after reading a ton of negative comments here, I decided to give my experience and rating. This place is not perfect, yes they need to work on some things and unfortunately not everyone will be helped sadly cuz that's unrealistic, but the lives they have impacted are huge including my own! They do this from the passion to end homelessness and everyday they strive to better serve clients which is what matters.

I don’t know what they do but they don’t answer emails, phone or voicemails… horrible. -5 stars….

Discriminate against black people and offer false hope with assistance. Told a client that they had an eviction which was not true, waste time so the voucher can not be used. I can only imagine how they treat others in need. I’m contacting BBB tomorrow, no worries.

name: Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity Office (HABC)
address: 410 E. Lexington St., Baltimore, MD 21202
description:
Terrible organization, Will fine you nonstop but you can't get in touch with anyone to discuss the reasoning. They go around and fine properties to make money rather than helping with solutions. They are quick to hit you with a fine but provide absolutely no help with issues in the city. Submitted multiple claims and they just brush them off with no solutions.

Do any of these numbers work? I've called and talked to people before however nothing has been resolved.  How do you confuse Owings Maryland with Owings Mills. Quiana Jones did not do her job properly and now my address is attached to a property title in Baltimore City I do not own. I have a court date for zero reason. These people just used google found someone with a similar name and filed court paperwork and altered titled. I'm not Michael Morgan Jr and I don't know no Pammy Morgan.

Then I call a random number finally get somebody they transfer me to a disconnect number. I call back they transfer me to the solicitor office. That person gives me two numbers. The first is supposedly for Aja Mallory. You get a message saying "this person doesn't take calls". The second number was for a supervisor and that number is disconnected.  What a joke.

I appreciate the quick response to my questions, my overall experience was great.
Mrs Brittany Dillon was very informative and helpful! Pleasant to talk to.

I live next to a 2 bedroom rental property that currently has 7 residents, 5 of which are adults. This is clearly a code violation, and a problematic situation for the surrounding neighbors and children residing there. Despite multiple 311 reports by multiple neighbors, the Housing Department refuses to enforce their code or even perform a proper inspection. If you search "Overcrowding" reports, there is basically zero enforcement, no matter how egregious the violation. I'm not sure if this is a deliberate decision by the city or just pure negligence, but it seems impossible to believe that no report anywhere in the last several months has resulted in a citation.

I spoke with Mr. Austin and another lady whose name escaped me. They were extremely helpful, and went above and beyond to get my property taken care of. It took me a few weeks but once I was able to reach them it was done right away.

I recommend everyone keep trying to call or just go in person. It’s worth the time.

name: Step-Up Program (HABC)
address: 410 E. Lexington St., Baltimore, MD 21202
description:
I’ve been a landlord partner with BRHP for nearly three years, and communication has been virtually nonexistent. Countless times, I’ve sent multiple emails that seem to disappear into the abyss—without even a brief acknowledgment such as, “We received your message.” This has happened across various departments and individuals, which suggests an overarching culture of poor communication and lack of responsiveness within BRHP.

I first learned of the agency when applicants began applying to my unit through the program. I will say this: my tenant has been excellent. Unfortunately, I cannot say the same about my experience communicating with the agency. It’s become so frustrating that I often contemplate ending my participation altogether. There appears to be little regard for urgency, accountability, or the value of program partners.

This nonprofit supposed to assist people with housing. I know someone whose housing in one of their apartments and thus is her first place ever, right. She got with them through HABC. She lost her job with no income and the back rent that was not picked up by HABC now she is being evicted. My you this person rent went to zero for the last six months or more.

I always go down there staff very nice ...diffently have to.be patient..they will help you im very thankful for everyone 🙏

As a landlord partner I can say.  Communication is horrible this non profit sure makes their "partners" jump through hoops.  Been trying to get documentation for about a month now.   Owners and PMs beware this is not a easy way to go.

Never call my wife back took her off the waiting list after being on there since 2017 she’s calling the news station so they can go have a interview in front of there  office This is very unprofessional and I hope they shut this whole program down and the people that works for this program She will be on Fox 45 news also 13 news

name: Housing Authority of Baltimore City (HABC)
address: 410 E. Lexington St., Baltimore, MD 21202
description:
Unprofessional, insensitive, and out of order. Its 2026 and Public Housing continues sending 4 letters to my deceased mother that passed away in 2023 after I informed the male worker over the phone twice in 2024. I put" return to sender, she is deceased" on all 4 mails addressed to my deceased mom and left two voice messages 2/13/26.

Super rude. If you have questions. Don't expect a clear concise answer. And if you get an answer it's coming with a side of rude. Thank you Debbie. You were sweet. To Debbie's supervisors. Ms Richardson and Mr Dennis. You guys are terrible, nasty people who do not need to work with the public. I understand the type of people you deal with. But we all aren't hood. Some of us are veterans elderly and disabled people who just need a place to live. You make people afraid to ask for help. You are there to help the public. Help them.

My homegirl did everything got her certification and everything now her case manager Mrs.Betts not answering or returning emails.

I disagree on how you collect your lease updates. I do not like the idea that you are collecting personal information from my bank account and my insurance company, I am 69 years old I disagree on sharing that with you because whats in my savings and checking account it for emergency only. I have been in public housing for 25 plus years, I feel like I own the place. I live longer in Pleasant View Garden 18 years and I never miss a payment and never late and always on time even before moving to that area. Dealing with poor conditions within the home. I dealt with it and fixed some of the problems on my own. Give me a good reason why and what you need it for. My situation has not changed. Thank You. Stay Safe and Take Care.

I'm not sure what's up with whom takes complete part on decisions pertaining to eligible applicants but waiting on a list for 9 years to finally getting my interview but still being told there isn't anything available is beyond ridiculous. I've seen And heard of many  getting pretty good options for housing and they make more then i do.

Taking a wild guess they're giving vouchers to people they know or possibly selling them . I've heard it all.

I just Thank God he makes a way every time bc you people are Absolutely Foul and Shady.  Hopefully tables don't turn on you one day Bc the way it's being mishandled isn't cool at all you will receive what you give.

name: Career T.R.A.C.K. (Indiana County)
address: 300 Indian Springs Road, Indiana, PA 15701
description:
I recommend this place to anyone who dropped out of high school and needs their GED or equivalent.

They offer monthly IndiGo bus passes via the career link program, where if you meet expectations, you can be offered a job and get paid to go to work and their classes.

I owe my entire career to this place, it taught me a lot about the working world.

I don’t recommend it because they aren’t realizable enough, they don’t normally give reasons to there actions to people, and they seem like they don’t treat people equally.

name: UC Board of Review
address: 651 Boas St. Room 625, Harrisburg, PA 17121
description:
Department if Labor and Industry? And they have no real person answering calls to help real people? Just an answering machine? This is what our taxes, OUR LABOR AND INDUSTRY pay for?

Great to know when the working folk need help, at least answering machines will be there for us.

The user interface of the system can be confusing and overwhelming, especially for first-time users.

I applied for benefits in Florida, but the process took over 10 weeks. When I realized I needed to file in Pennsylvania, it took another 3+ weeks to withdraw my Florida application.

To make things worse, wait times in Florida can be anywhere from 2-3 hours. On a good day, you might get offered a callback, but that can take another 3 hours. And even then, the callback might not happen at all.
In contrast to my experience in Florida, the employees in Pennsylvania have been incredibly helpful and supportive throughout this difficult process. I would like to thank each and every one of them for their hard work, dedication, and compassion.

However, I must deduct one star from my rating due to the complexity of the program and the multiple portals that need to be navigated. During an already stressful time, the system can be unnecessarily difficult to understand and use.

Despite this minor setback, I want to express my sincere gratitude to the employees in Pennsylvania for their outstanding service. They truly care about their fellow Pennsylvanians and go above and beyond to assist those in need.

I know this is a frustrating time for all and I am in the foxhole with you.  The system may need improvement but the people are fantastic!! I appreciate you PA EMPLOYEES!!!!

Nightmare  in 2020 Nightmare in 2025 Gov Shapiro said "UC has improved."
Delusional at best

Never can get through to a human. Call all day for months on end and still nothing. Then I finally thought I got through one day and it was an automated message that hung up on me. No options, no help. Their live chat feature is also down and has been offline for almost a year now! Fix this issue, it’s not my fault the government shut everything down and my place of employment had to close permanently. I haven’t gotten anything in 6 months and nothing before that for at least 4 months. RIDICULOUS.

I filed in March and got my pin in June. My UC claim says there is an issue and that payment can not be made until the issue is resolved. I just want to know what I need to do to resolve this issue.... There’s no way to get a call through. No one to speak with. Emails never get a reply. I’ve been on the “live” chat waiting for weeks and still no response.

I can only hope that this review, as well as all the other reviews here, serve as fuel for this labor department to change for the better.

name: Department of Corrections
address: Technology Parkway, Mechanicsburg, PA 17050
